kata.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
kata.workload: one-pass O(n) max-profit scan (track min-so-far, best = max(prices[k]-min)); N=2,000,000 LCG-generated prices in [1,4096], K=10 outer calls, sum-of-results sink

env.note: Measured on a shared x86-64 Linux cloud container (karac from kara main), NOT the corpus's Apple M5 Pro. Canonical M5 numbers in the sibling results.json are left untouched (same K=10 workload). Absolute times/sizes/RSS NOT comparable across the two files; only within-file cross-language ratios are the signal. This is a SHORT workload (~25-33ms) so the container noise is elevated (rust/c stddev up to ~20%); read ratios, not absolute ms — the M5 run is the stable/canonical one. Seq lane (fastest first): rust 24.7ms < c ~30ms ~ go 31.2ms ~ rust_ovf 32.0ms ~ kara 32.5ms. RESULT: kara is at EQUAL-SAFETY PARITY with rust_ovf (both ~32ms, ~1.3x behind rust -O). The ~1.3x vs rust -O is the OVERFLOW-CHECK tax, not a codegen gap: kara's scan is branchless (cmovl min / cmovg best), bounds-check-elided (the `for i in 1..n` forward index folds), unrolled 2x, with one jo overflow check on `prices[k]-min` — the same shape rust_ovf carries. All mirrors agree on the sink; correctness verified across interp/JIT/AOT/auto-par. Python (K=10, own timing, not cross-checked) omitted. Re-run bench.sh on the M5 to refresh the canonical table.
